title: Quantum Linguistics
subtitle: The Advanced Language Patterns of NLP
description: You can directionalize your language to affect internal representations. Neurological changes can be made using directionalized language. You can control the client's internal representations during conversation.

embeddedCommands.title: Embedded Commands
embeddedCommands.subtitle: How to Deliver Ones That Work!

twoElements.element1: 2 times (or 2 words) MEANS 'How to do it.'

embeddedCommands.key: The key is three in a single sentence. Delivered LOWER & LOUDER!
steps: Decide Outcome
Develop Sentence
Deliver It
Calibrate for Results -- Outcome

quadrants.name: Theorem
quadrants.question: What would happen if you did?

quadrants.name: Converse
quadrants.question: What wouldn't happen if you did?

quadrants.name: Inverse
quadrants.question: What would happen if you didn't?

quadrants.name: Non-Mirror Image Reverse
quadrants.question: What wouldn't happen if you didn't?

deduction.example: Since I can't drive any car, I won't be able to drive a Cadillac.

induction.example: If I can learn to do this, I can learn anything.

hierarchyOfIdeas.title: Hierarchy of Ideas
hierarchyOfIdeas.subtitle: The level of abstraction in your language makes a difference

chunkUp.purpose: Chunk up to gain greater agreement, and trance.
questions: What is this an Example of?
chunkUp.result: Big Picture, Milton Model, Abstract, In Trance, Intuitor, Agreement

chunkDown.purpose: Chunk down for greater distinctions, and to bring them out of trance.
chunkDown.result: Specific, Meta Model, Details, Out of Trance, Sensor, Distinctions

hierarchyOfIdeas.structureOfIntuition: The ability to chunk-up to find connections & relationships, and then to chunk back down to relate to the current situation.

nlpNegotiationModel.title: NLP Negotiation Model
steps: Discover the positions of each of the parties in the conflict. Make sure agreement is possible. Make sure that both parties can make a decision.

Begin with one side (generally the least solid) and Chunk up - until you get beyond the boundaries of what the position originally was. You will know this when the original position becomes meaningless.
Separate intention from behavior. Use a conditional close: 'So if you get X then however we do it is OK.'
Chunk down only as quickly as you can maintain agreement. If maintenance of agreement is not possible, then chunk objecting party higher.

distortions.name: Mind Reading
distortions.description: Claiming to know someone's internal state.
distortions.response: How do you know I don't like you?

distortions.name: Lost Performative
distortions.description: Value judgments where the person doing the judging is left out.
distortions.response: Who says it's bad? According to whom? How do you know it's bad.
distortions.prediction: Gathers evidence. Recovers source of the belief, the Performative, strategy for the belief.

distortions.name: Cause-Effect
distortions.description: Where cause is wrongly put outside the self.
distortions.response: How does what I'm doing cause you to choose to feel sad? (Also, Counter Ex., or How Specifically?)

distortions.name: Complex Equivalence
distortions.description: Where two experiences are interpreted as being synonymous.
distortions.example: She's always yelling at me, she doesn't like me.
distortions.response: How does her yelling mean that she...? Have you ever yelled at someone you liked?

distortions.name: Presuppositions
distortions.example: If my husband knew how much I suffered, he wouldn't do that.
distortions.response: (1) How do you choose to suffer? (2) How is he (re)acting? (3) How do you know he doesn't know?
distortions.prediction: Specify the choice & the verb, & what he does. Recover the Internal Rep., and the Complex Equivalence

generalizations.name: Universal Quantifiers
generalizations.description: Universal Generalizations such as all, every, never, everyone, no one, etc.
generalizations.response: Find Counter Examples. 'Never?' 'What would happen if she did?'
generalizations.prediction: Recovers Counter Examples, Effects, and Outcomes.

generalizations.name: Modal Operators of Necessity
generalizations.description: As in should, shouldn't, must, must not, have to, need to, it is necessary.
generalizations.example: I have to take care of her.
generalizations.response: What would happen if you did? (What would happen if you didn't?) Also, 'Or?'

generalizations.name: Modal Operators of Possibility
generalizations.description: (Or Impossibility.) As in can/can't, will/won't, may/may not, possible/impossible.
generalizations.example: I can't tell him the truth.
generalizations.response: What prevents you? (What would happen if you did?)

deletions.name: Nominalizations
deletions.description: Process words which have been frozen in time, making them nouns.
deletions.response: Who's not communicating what to whom? How would you like to communicate?
deletions.prediction: Turns it back into a process, recovers deletion, and Ref. Index.

deletions.name: Lack of Referential Index
deletions.description: Fails to specify a person or thing.
deletions.response: Who, specifically, doesn't listen to you?

deletions.name: Comparative Deletions
deletions.description: As in good, better, best, worst, more, less, most, least.
deletions.response: Better than whom? Better at what? Compared to whom, what?

metaModelIII.title: Meta Model III - Directed Questioning for a Specific Result

steps.question: How have you failed to resolve this?
How can you overcome the solution to your problem?
What would you like to change?
When will you STOP IT from being a limitation?
How many ways do you know you have solved this?
I know you are changing and seeing things differently.

advancedPresuppositions.title: Advanced Presuppositions - The Process
advancedPresuppositions.description: The purpose of this process is to learn how to just loosen the client's model, not necessarily to solve the problem (which you may do anyway), by undercutting the original presuppositions.
steps: Identify the most basic presuppositions (write them down). Ask: 'How is this a problem now?' Identify ALL presuppositions in the sentence.
Determine which one(s) will have the greatest effect on the problem.
Structure response as a question, and presuppose a solution in the question. (How? By doing a Mind Read on what solution will work and asking a question that presupposes it.)
Bring the presuppositions into consciousness (restate the problem).
Ask the question.

decisionDestroyer.title: Decision Destroyer
decisionDestroyer.context: Where client says, 'I've already decided.' This process requires rapport!
steps: Pace the problem. Get the client back to the decision.
Where were you when you were deciding that?
.....just before that where were you?
NOW, as you think about your present situation in life, notice how many options you have, now.
Think of that problem and notice how you feel now.
As you think about the next time you may do X, knowing what you know now, notice how much better you feel, not doing it.

goingBeyondBoundaries.title: Going Beyond Boundaries
goingBeyondBoundaries.subtitle: It's a good thing to NOT Know
steps: What is it?
What is it not?
How do you know? (Re: What it is not.)
What is it that you need to not know to know this?
goingBeyondBoundaries.alternative: What is it that you are pretending to not know to know this?

linguisticReSourcing.title: Linguistic Re-Sourcing
steps: What's the problem?
How do you know (it's a problem)?
When did you decide that?
When don't you do it now?
What are you not deciding when you don't do it?
How is that different from how you were?
How do you know that, now?
What other changes would you like to make?

deIdentificationPattern.title: De-Identification Pattern
deIdentificationPattern.description: This pattern is useful for going beyond limiting (and therefore false) identifications, usually in the form of a Complex Equivalence. Complex equivalence's are the language construction of identification.
steps: Elicit the identification in the form of a Complex Equivalence. Listen for: the verb 'to be' or 'Means'
Pace and feedback the complex equivalence. 'So, you are ___.'
'Is that all you think you are?' (Look for a physiological shift.)
'Aren't you more than that?' (There should be agreement.)
'So, what are you that's not (the previous identification)?' You want a verbal answer from the client.
'And beyond (the word elicited in #5), is that all you are? How much more are you than that?' For extra leverage use Tag Question: 'You do know you are more than that, don't you?'
'How do you know?' Anchors the change to the client's reality strategy.

linguisticPartsIntegration.title: Linguistic Parts Integration
linguisticPartsIntegration.description: This pattern can be useful in integrating Parts. Here's how you would resolve a Part's conflict using Prime Concerns.
steps: Discover the Function/Behavior for Each Part: 'What is the behavior of the Part, or what is the function that the Part plays?'
Discover the Purpose/Intention of the Behavior: 'What is the purpose of this behavior? What is the intent of the behavior?'
Make sure the Learning's are Preserved: 'What is there to learn from the behavior?' (Handle the ecology of the integration.)

Switch the Purpose/Intention of One Part to the other: Take the intention from part A and talk about it as though it was the intention of part B, while at the same time talking about the original intention for Part A and the original intention for Part B.
Test for Integration

elicitation.title: Prime Concerns Elicitation - Starting, Changing, Stopping
elicitation.description: For our purposes in elicitation there are six possibilities that operate in conjunction with one another. In other words, a person will usually be best at one and worst at one. So there are two questions to use, and they should be asked in order.

questions.label: First Question
questions.question: What are you best at -- starting things, changing things, or stopping things?

questions.label: Second Question
questions.question: What are you worst at -- starting things, changing things, or stopping things?

questions.label: Third Question
questions.question: So (who/what) is it that you are not (being/doing/having) that you want to (be/do/have)?

predictions: The person who's worst at starting things will have trouble being who they want to be. 'Why can't I be who I want?'
The person who's worst at changing things will have trouble doing what they want to do. 'Why can't I do what I want to do?'
The person that's worst at stopping things will have trouble having what they want. 'Why can't I ever get what I want?'

process.title: Prime Concerns - Overview of the Process
steps: Gather Information Re: The Presenting Problem (Starting, Changing, Stopping / Ecstatic State)
Listen/Look for: Significant, Analogically Marked Words
Test Words: (Use the Same Words) Feed them back
See If the Neural Networks are Activated
Generate a New Pattern: In one of the Cartesian Coordinate quadrants, And Inductive... Have them Fully Consider the New Pattern!
Use the pattern to go back and forth across the threshold & the boundaries -- 3x or 7x or 21x.
Resolution: The Problem Disappears / They Can't Hear You / Parts Integrate / Boundary Disappears / Capacity of words to act as linguistic marker disappears
Test and Future Pace

nullSet.title: Prime Concerns Null Set
steps: Tell me about that ....
Tell me more .... (until client has said everything that (s)he can think of)
What is it that you haven't told me that you're NOT thinking of ... right now! (Anchor)
Then fire off the Null Set anchor as they consider problems, problem states, or strategies that create the problem states.

reality.title: Reality
howWeConstructReality: We make measurements through our language.

timeScramble.title: Time Scramble - An Example of Temporal Language
examples: Go inside and try in vain to have the same problem.
It was a terrible problem, wasn't it?
You want to make changes haven't you?...
What would it be like when you have made those changes, now?

In the future as you look back and see what it was like to have had that problem...as you think about it now, if you could make this change for yourself so that you could STOP...having made that change and see yourself now.
Do you like the way you look if you could make that change and look back at yourself having made that change now!

prepositions.title: Prepositions
prepositions.definition: A preposition is a word that connects a verb and a noun by indicating a relationship between the things for which they stand. Prepositions limit verbs (much like adverbs) as to some circumstance of space, time, manner, degree, cause or reason.
